PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO PRIMARY RESIDENCE
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Approve Settlement
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alexis Levy appeared via zoom for Fabiola Rachel (petitioner).
James Millar appeared via zoom for John Gallion (objector).

From the outset of the hearing Mr. Millar stated that he doesn't oppose the petition. The matters are taken under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Michael McNaughton. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Obi. Not reported.

Petition To Approve Settlement

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Feb-23-2026 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Nov-17-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alexis B. Levy for petitioner Fabiola Rachal (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Amended Petition to Determine Succession to Primary Residence; and 2) Status of Mediation.

Counsel reported that mediation had been successful. They stated that a written settlement agreement had been drafted and all signatories on their side had executed it. A few remaining signatures from objecting parties remained outstanding, but counsel anticipated receiving them within approximately one week.

Counsel stated they would be filing a petition for approval of the settlement once all signatures were in place.

The Court raised the issue of whether the instant petition, as framed, would fully support implementation of the settlement, particularly where all heirs agree that distribution shall be 100% to petitioner. The Court suggested that a different petition format might be cleaner. The Court continued the matter and set a hearing on status of petition to approve settlement for February 23, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Counsel was encouraged to evaluate a practical path forward on the current petition.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO PRIMARY RESIDENCE
Off Calendar Nov-17-2025 Continued To Feb-23-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO PRIMARY RESIDENCE
Off Calendar Aug-27-2025 Continued To Nov-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alexis Levy for and with petitioner and heir Fabiola Rachal, who was also present.
James Millar for objector and heir John Gallion.
Additional heirs Tomas Llorence, Valeria Llorence, Adelise Gallion, and Anne E. Murray were also present in court.

The matter came on calendar for hearing on petitioner's request to confirm decedent's property interest.

The Court noted deficiencies in the petition as identified by the probate examiner, including lack of clarity regarding the percentage interest sought and inconsistencies in the moving papers.

Mr. Levy argued that the heirs of law and factual background were largely uncontested, and that petitioner's position was supported by historical valuations. Mr. Millar for objector argued that the petition did not adequately set forth how the percentage interest was calculated and that the relief requested was unclear.

The Court observed that the petition was not in a state where it could be granted and encouraged the parties to meet and confer to see if issues could be resolved. Both sides expressed a willingness to mediate.

Following discussion, the Court signed a Minute Order Referring Case to Pro Bono Mediation ordering that mediation must be completed by October 31, 2025. Counsel shall notify the probate examiner of the mediation outcome on or before November 7, 2025. Hearing on status of mediation is set for November 17, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The parties were instructed to obtain a copy of the signed order from the Court's website.

The Court continued the matter to November 17, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
